steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: The blank fills the slot before “findings” and must function as a possessive determiner referring to “Watson and Crick” — a plural noun phrase. The correct word must be a plural possessive, not a contraction.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'They’re': Violates possessive vs. contraction — “they’re” is a contraction for “they are,” not a possessive determiner, so it produces a grammatically broken sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'It’s': Violates possessive vs. contraction — “it’s” is a contraction for “it is” or “it has,” not a possessive determiner, and also does not agree with the plural antecedent. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Their': “Their” is the plural possessive determiner, correctly agreeing in number with the plural antecedent “Watson and Crick.” ✓
  • Choice D — 'Its': Violates pronoun-antecedent agreement — “its” is a singular possessive determiner and does not agree with the plural “Watson and Crick.” ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
